data.narrative:
Rigetti Computing is a publicly traded quantum computing company valued at $5.5B, operating within a sector comprising 3 public peers (IonQ, D-Wave Quantum, Infleqtion) and 8 private quantum firms. The company has raised over $300M to date under founder Chad Rigetti.

Peer-median valuation analysis across four same-sector, same-stage comparables yields an implied valuation of $1.9B. The peer set spans a wide range—from Archer Materials at $60M to IonQ at $11.0B—reflecting heterogeneous market assessments of quantum computing maturity and commercialization timelines. The median of $1.9B represents the midpoint of this distribution, with D-Wave at $2.0B and Infleqtion at $1.8B bracketing the central tendency.

The triangulation analysis, based on the single applicable method, supports a valuation range of $1.6B (low) to $2.2B (median to high). Rigetti's current $5.5B valuation sits materially above this range, implying either market pricing of differentiated technology, intellectual property, or near-term commercial pathway not captured in peer multiples. Key uncertainties include the timing of fault-tolerant quantum computing deployment, relative performance of competing qubit modalities, and durability of first-mover advantages. The wide dispersion among public comparables ($60M to $11.0B) signals substantial disagreement in the market regarding valuation fundamentals across the sector.
